chief_complaint: An 82-year-old patient presented with severe acute cervicalgia, fever, arthritis of the wrists and hands.
history: The patient exhibited symptoms mimicking acute meningitis, including elevated inflammatory parameters. Acute meningitis was ruled out with a normal cerebrospinal fluid study.
exam: Vital signs included fever. Physical examination revealed severe cervicalgia, arthritis of the wrists and hands.
labs: Computed tomography scan of the cervical spine revealed calcifications around the odontoid process. Cerebrospinal fluid study was normal.
differentials: Calcium pyrophosphate dihydrate crystal deposition disease
correct_ranking: Calcium pyrophosphate dihydrate crystal deposition disease
